name: quality-reviewer
description: Automatically reviews DevPrep AI code for quality standards including ESLint compliance, TypeScript strict mode, 180-line file limits, complexity under 15, proper naming conventions, import patterns, and architectural compliance with the 6-folder structure
capabilities: Check file size limits (180 lines maximum per file)
Verify cyclomatic complexity under 15 per function
Validate TypeScript naming conventions with I prefix for interfaces
Detect incorrect import patterns requiring path aliases
Run ESLint and TypeScript compiler checks
author: ArieGoldkin
securitySummary: This is a legitimate code quality review skill. The 262 static findings are all false positives: markdown backticks flagged as shell commands, example bad code showing what NOT to do, and documentation about deprecated practices. The actual bash scripts only run standard dev tools (npm, grep, find, wc) scoped to frontend/src. No network calls to external endpoints, no credential access, and no persistence mechanisms.
valueStatement: Manual code reviews take time and miss issues. This skill runs 7 automated checks for file size, complexity, naming, imports, architecture, ESLint, and TypeScript compliance with DevPrep AI standards.
limitations: Designed specifically for DevPrep AI project structure
Only scans TypeScript and TSX files in frontend/src directory
Requires npm and npx to be available in PATH

useCases.title: Pre-commit quality gate
useCases.description: Integrate into pre-commit hooks to catch standards violations before code enters the repository.

useCases.title: Automated review feedback
useCases.description: Generate consistent quality reports for every pull request without manual checklist tracking.

useCases.title: Standards onboarding
useCases.description: Help new team members learn project conventions through immediate, actionable feedback.

promptTemplates.title: Quick review
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need a fast quality check
promptTemplates.prompt: Review the code I just wrote for quality standards compliance

promptTemplates.title: Full audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Run a full quality review using all checks on the frontend/src directory

promptTemplates.title: Specific check
promptTemplates.prompt: Check file sizes and complexity in the modules directory

promptTemplates.title: Fix guidance
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need help resolving violations
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to fix the naming convention violations and explain the patterns

bestPractices: Run full reviews before committing to catch all issues at once
Address violations incrementally when learning the standards
Use specific checks (check-file-size, check-naming) for focused feedback
antiPatterns: Ignoring violations and pushing code anyway
Running only one check when multiple standards are likely violated

faq.question: Which AI platforms support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It uses Bash, Read, Grep, and Glob tools available on all platforms.

faq.question: What are the line and complexity limits?
faq.answer: Maximum 180 lines of code per file, maximum 15 cyclomatic complexity per function, maximum 50 lines per function.

faq.question: How do I integrate with pre-commit hooks?
faq.answer: Add ./.claude/skills/quality-reviewer/scripts/full-review.sh to your .husky/pre-commit file to run checks before each commit.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my code?
faq.answer: No, this skill only reads and analyzes code. It reports violations but does not make any changes to your files.

faq.question: Why are my imports being flagged?
faq.answer: The skill requires path aliases (@shared/, @modules/, @lib/, @store/) instead of relative imports (../../../) for better maintainability.

faq.question: How is this different from ESLint?
faq.answer: ESLint catches syntax and style issues. This skill enforces project-specific architectural standards like folder structure, file organization, and naming conventions.
